channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Deadly attack on Pakistan outpost puts Afghanistan ceasefire at risk
item.description: Two assaults in the northwest region have killed more than 20 people in recent days.

item.title: Israeli settler attacks activist during tour on settler violence
item.description: A group of Israeli settlers attacked Israeli rights activists in the occupied West Bank.

item.title: Lukaku included in Belgium’s World Cup squad despite injury-plagued season
item.description: The country's top goalscorer has not started for Napoli this season and made just seven substitute appearances.

item.title: Why the Iran conflict is becoming a problem for BRICS
item.description: Second BRICS meeting in a row collapses without joint position because of disagreements over the war on Iran.

item.title: David Beckham becomes first British billionaire sportsman
item.description: Beckham and his wife Victoria's collective wealth reached the milestone this year, according to Sunday Times Rich List.

item.title: Health officials raise alarm over new Ebola outbreak in DR Congo
item.description: Concern is high regarding the potential spread of the virus amid a precarious security situation in the affected area.

item.title: Finland ends drone alert amid regional fears of Ukraine war spillover
item.description: Finnish defence authorities scramble fighter jets; defence chief warns of more alarms as Russian war persists.

item.title: This is the Palestinian Nakba, measured in land taken
item.description: On May 15, Palestinians mark 78 years since the Nakba. The count of land Israel has taken keeps growing today.

item.title: India hikes fuel prices as Iran crisis bites
item.description: The world's third-largest oil importer announced increased fuel prices and austerity measures due to the energy crisis.

item.title: Three years of the West Bank’s ‘third Nakba’
item.description: Displacement and violence haunt Palestinian Bedouins, forced from their homes repeatedly since the Nakba of 1948.

item.title: Trump and Xi move towards business-first relationship after Beijing summit
item.description: The two leaders did not resolve differences on key issues like Iran and Taiwan, despite push to extend truce.

item.title: How Xi-Trump summit failed to yield Iran war breakthrough
item.description: US officials have nudged China to do more on the blocked Hormuz strait. President Xi doesn't appear to have budged.

item.title: Xi praises US ‘milestone’ visit and relationship, offers Trump roses
item.description: Xi praised the new 'constructive and strategic' relationship with the US, before Trump concluded his China trip.

item.title: US seeks indictment of former Cuban leader Raul Castro
item.description: The potential charges relate to Cuba's 1996 downing of humanitarian planes, US media outlets report.

item.title: Nadim Bawalsa & the Palestinian diaspora: From denial to genocide
item.description: Nadim Bawalsa traces how early Palestinian migrants in Latin America forged national identity long before 1948.

item.title: Scientists identify massive new dinosaur following Thailand dig
item.description: Nagatitan chaiyaphumensis had enough unique features to be considered a new species.

item.title: Benched Mbappe says he’s fourth-choice forward at Real Madrid under Arbeloa
item.description: Striker jeered by own fans as he came off bench in return from injury in 2-0 La Liga win ​over Real Oviedo.

item.title: Nakba Day: 78 years of ethnic cleansing
item.description: On May 15, Palestinians across historic Palestine and the diaspora commemorate the ‘Nakba’ or the ‘catastrophe’ of 1948.

item.title: Millions of Palestinians mark 78 years since the Nakba
item.description: Nakba commemorations serve as remembrance and a call for justice, self-determination and the right to return.

item.title: Mitoma fails to make Japan’s 2026 World Cup squad due to hamstring injury
item.description: Monaco forward Takumi Minamino also misses out due to injury, but ex-Arsenal defender Takehiro Tomiyasu makes the cut.

item.title: How David Ben-Gurion got the Palestinians wrong in 1948
item.description: The Palestinian question never disappeared despite Israel’s best efforts and worst brutality.

item.title: Iran war day 77: Trump, Xi discuss Hormuz as Tehran rallies BRICS
item.description: The US and Chinese leaders agreed during talks that the Strait of Hormuz should remain open to ensure global supplies.

item.title: Why India’s RSS is lobbying the West amid attacks on minorities at home
item.description: Hate speech against religious minorities in India rose last year. Experts say far-right RSS is doing 'damage control'.

item.title: Trump and Xi hold high-stakes talks in Beijing
item.description: US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping are meeting for a second day in Beijing.

item.title: Top US admiral: Strikes severely degraded Iran’s military, defence
item.description: Admiral Brad Cooper defended the results of the US-Israeli war on Iran, as lawmakers questioned him on Capitol Hill.
